LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARJORIE HEINRICH, ETHAN WIMERT AND HEINRICH LAW, PC'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO ALL SIX CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL SIX CAUSES OF ACTION. ALL SIX CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED AND DO NOT CONTRAVENE THE SHAM PLEADING DOCTRINE. THE FRAUD CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ALLEGED WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY. A PLEADING ALLEGING A BREACH OF A WRITTEN CONTRACT NEED NOT ATTACH THE CONTRACT OR STATE ALL THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT VERBATIM. IT IS SUFFICIENT IF THE PLEADING ALLEGES THE LEGAL OF THE CONTRACT, WHICH PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER DOLAN HAS DONE. (CONSTRUCTION PROTECTION SERVICES, INC. V. TIG SPECIALTY INS. CO. (2002) 29 CAL.4TH 189, 198-199.) NONE OF THE SIX CAUSES OF ACTION ON THEIR FACE NECESSARILY VIOLATES CALIFORNIA PUBLIC POLICY, BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE 6068(N) OR 16600 OR CRPC 3-700(D)(1). MR. DOLAN HAS ALLEGED COGNIZABLE DAMAGES FOR EACH OF HIS CLAIMS. MR. DOLAN HAS ALSO SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED THE INFORMATION THAT HE CONTENDS CONSTITUTES TRADE SECRETS WHICH MEETS THE STATUTORY DEFINITION OF TRADE SECRETS. FURTHER SPECIFICITY CAN BE OBTAINED THROUGH DISCOVERY. PARAGRAPHS 110-113 OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT TRACK THE LANGUAGE OF PENAL CODE 502(C)(1), (C)(4) AND (C)(7) AND THEREFORE PROVIDE SUFFICIENT NOTICE AS TO THE STATUTORY BASES FOR THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. WHILE IT IS QUITE POSSIBLE THAT SOME OR ALL OF MR. DOLAN'S CLAIMS LACK FACTUAL AND/OR LEGAL SUPPORT, THAT DETERMINATION MUST BE MADE BASED ON THE EVIDENCE. THE SIX CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED IN CONFORMITY WITH APPLICABLE PLEADING REQUIREMENTS TO WARRANT OVERRULING THE DEMURRER AS TO EACH OF THEM. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Off Calendar Sep-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER DOLAN's Motion To Quash Subpoena to SF Examiner is GRANTED in part. All portions of the subpoena that seek non-public financial information of either Mr. Dolan or the Examiner is quashed. The motion is DENIED as to all other information sought by the subpoena. The only information sought by the subpoena that implicates either Mr. Dolan's or the Examiner's privacy rights is non-public financial information such as the amounts paid by Mr. Dolan for advertising or any other financial arrangement he may have with the Examiner and the amounts requested or received by the Examiner. Defendants have failed to show that non-public financial information is directly relevant to Mr. Dolan's claims or their defenses. All information other than non-public financial information is subject to the usual standard of discovery relevance and defendants have shown that all such information satisfies that standard. The moving party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Matthew D. Gramly of The Dolan Law Firm (415.421.2800) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS MARJORIE HEINRICH, ETHAN WIMERT AND HEINRICH LAW, PC'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all six causes of action in the second amended complaint is OVERRULED as to all six causes of action. All six causes of action are adequately alleged and do not contravene the sham pleading doctrine. The fraud causes of action are alleged with sufficient particularity. A pleading alleging a breach of a written contract need not attach the contract or state all the terms of the contract verbatim. It is sufficient if the pleading alleges the legal of the contract, which plaintiff Christopher Dolan has done. (Construction Protection Services, Inc. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 198-199.) None of the six causes of action on their face necessarily violates California public policy, Business and Professions Code 6068(n) or 16600 or CRPC 3-700(D)(1). Mr. Dolan has alleged cognizable damages for each of his claims. Mr. Dolan has also sufficiently alleged the information that he contends constitutes trade secrets which meets the statutory definition of trade secrets. Further specificity can be obtained through discovery. Paragraphs 110-113 of the second amended complaint track the language of Penal Code 502(c)(1), (c)(4) and (c)(7) and therefore provide sufficient notice as to the statutory bases for the sixth cause of action. While it is quite possible that some or all of Mr. Dolan's claims lack factual and/or legal support, that determination must be made based on the evidence. The six causes of action are sufficiently alleged in conformity with applicable pleading requirements to warrant overruling the demurrer as to each of them. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)
